verses.kjv: IN the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
verses.bsb: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.kjv: The same was in the beginning with God.
verses.bsb: He was with God in the beginning.

verses.kjv: All things were made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
verses.bsb: Through Him all things were made, and without Him nothing was made that has been made.

verses.kjv: In him was life; and the life was the light of men.
verses.bsb: In Him was life, and that life was the light of men.

verses.kjv: And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.
verses.bsb: The Light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.

verses.kjv: ¶ There was a man sent from God, whose name was John.
verses.bsb: There came a man who was sent from God. His name was John.

verses.kjv: The same came for a witness, to bear witness of the Light, that all men through him might believe.
verses.bsb: He came as a witness to testify about the Light, so that through him everyone might believe.

verses.kjv: He was not that Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
verses.bsb: He himself was not the Light, but he came to testify about the Light.

verses.kjv: That was the true Light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.
verses.bsb: The true Light who gives light to every man was coming into the world.

verses.kjv: He was in the world, and the world was made by him, and the world knew him not.
verses.bsb: He was in the world, and though the world was made through Him, the world did not recognize Him.

verses.kjv: He came unto his own, and his own received him not.
verses.bsb: He came to His own, and His own did not receive Him.

verses.kjv: But as many as received him, to them gave he power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on his name:
verses.bsb: But to all who did receive Him, to those who believed in His name, He gave the right to become children of God—

verses.kjv: Which were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.
verses.bsb: children born not of blood, nor of the desire or will of man, but born of God.

verses.kjv: And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us, (and we beheld his glory, the glory as of the only begotten of the Father,) full of grace and truth.
verses.bsb: The Word became flesh and made His dwelling among us. We have seen His glory, the glory of the one and only Son from the Father, full of grace and truth.

verses.kjv: ¶ John bare witness of him, and cried, saying, This was he of whom I spake, He that cometh after me is preferred before me: for he was before me.
verses.bsb: John testified concerning Him. He cried out, saying, “This is He of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me because He was before me.’”

verses.kjv: And of his fulness have all we received, and grace for grace.
verses.bsb: From His fullness we have all received grace upon grace.

verses.kjv: For the law was given by Moses, but grace and truth came by Jesus Christ.
verses.bsb: For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.

verses.kjv: No man hath seen God at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath declared him.
verses.bsb: No one has ever seen God, but the one and only Son, who is Himself God and is at the Father’s side, has made Him known.

verses.kjv: ¶ And this is the record of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?
verses.bsb: And this was John’s testimony when the Jews of Jerusalem sent priests and Levites to ask him, “Who are you?”

verses.kjv: And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.
verses.bsb: He did not refuse to confess, but openly declared, “I am not the Christ.”

verses.kjv: And they asked him, What then? Art thou Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou that prophet? And he answered, No.
verses.bsb: “Then who are you?” they inquired. “Are you Elijah?”  He said, “I am not.”  “Are you the Prophet?”  He answered, “No.”

verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?
verses.bsb: So they said to him, “Who are you? We need an answer for those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”

verses.kjv: He said, I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make straight the way of the Lord, as said the prophet Esaias.
verses.bsb: John replied in the words of Isaiah the prophet:  “I am a voice of one calling in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way for the Lord.’”

verses.kjv: And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees who had been sent

verses.kjv: And they asked him, and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not that Christ, nor Elias, neither that prophet?
verses.bsb: asked him, “Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”

verses.kjv: John answered them, saying, I baptize with water: but there standeth one among you, whom ye know not;
verses.bsb: “I baptize with water,” John replied, “but among you stands One you do not know.

verses.kjv: He it is, who coming after me is preferred before me, whose shoe’s latchet I am not worthy to unloose.
verses.bsb: He is the One who comes after me, the straps of whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.”

verses.kjv: These things were done in Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.
verses.bsb: All this happened at Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.

verses.kjv: ¶ The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world.
verses.bsb: The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!

verses.kjv: This is he of whom I said, After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: for he was before me.
verses.bsb: This is He of whom I said, ‘A man who comes after me has surpassed me because He was before me.’

verses.kjv: And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel, therefore am I come baptizing with water.
verses.bsb: I myself did not know Him, but the reason I came baptizing with water was that He might be revealed to Israel.”

verses.kjv: And John bare record, saying, I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove, and it abode upon him.
verses.bsb: Then John testified, “I saw the Spirit descending from heaven like a dove and resting on Him.

verses.kjv: And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he which baptizeth with the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: I myself did not know Him, but the One who sent me to baptize with water told me, ‘The man on whom you see the Spirit descend and rest is He who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.’

verses.kjv: And I saw, and bare record that this is the Son of God.
verses.bsb: I have seen and testified that this is the Son of God.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;
verses.bsb: The next day John was there again with two of his disciples.

verses.kjv: And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the Lamb of God!
verses.bsb: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Look, the Lamb of God!”

verses.kjv: And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
verses.bsb: And when the two disciples heard him say this, they followed Jesus.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?
verses.bsb: Jesus turned and saw them following. “What do you want?” He asked.  They said to Him, “Rabbi” (which means Teacher), “where are You staying?”

verses.kjv: He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
verses.bsb: “Come and see,” He replied. So they went and saw where He was staying, and spent that day with Him. It was about the tenth hour.

verses.kjv: One of the two which heard John speak, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
verses.bsb: Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, was one of the two who heard John’s testimony and followed Jesus.

verses.kjv: He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.
verses.bsb: He first found his brother Simon and told him, “We have found the Messiah” (which is translated as Christ).

verses.kjv: And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.
verses.bsb: Andrew brought him to Jesus, who looked at him and said, “You are Simon son of John. You will be called Cephas” (which is translated as Peter).

verses.kjv: ¶ The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.
verses.bsb: The next day Jesus decided to set out for Galilee. Finding Philip, He told him, “Follow Me.”

verses.kjv: Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
verses.bsb: Now Philip was from Bethsaida, the same town as Andrew and Peter.

verses.kjv: Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, did write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.
verses.bsb: Philip found Nathanael and told him, “We have found the One Moses wrote about in the Law, the One the prophets foretold—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.”

verses.kjv: And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.
verses.bsb: “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Nathanael asked.  “Come and see,” said Philip.

verses.kjv: Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile!
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, He said of him, “Here is a true Israelite, in whom there is no deceit.”

verses.kjv: Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.
verses.bsb: “How do You know me?” Nathanael asked.  Jesus replied, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”

verses.kjv: Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the Son of God; thou art the King of Israel.
verses.bsb: “Rabbi,” Nathanael answered, “You are the Son of God! You are the King of Israel!”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to him, “Do you believe just because I told you I saw you under the fig tree? You will see greater things than these.”

verses.kjv: And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.
verses.bsb: Then He declared, “Truly, truly, I tell you, you will all see heaven open and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”

verses.kjv: AND the third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:
verses.bsb: On the third day a wedding took place at Cana in Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there,

verses.kjv: And both Jesus was called, and his disciples, to the marriage.
verses.bsb: and Jesus and His disciples had also been invited to the wedding.

verses.kjv: And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.
verses.bsb: When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to Him, “They have no more wine.”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.
verses.bsb: “Woman, why does this concern us?” Jesus replied. “My hour has not yet come.”

verses.kjv: His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it.
verses.bsb: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever He tells you.”

verses.kjv: And there were set there six waterpots of stone, after the manner of the purifying of the Jews, containing two or three firkins apiece.
verses.bsb: Now six stone water jars had been set there for the Jewish rites of purification. Each could hold from twenty to thirty gallons.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Fill the waterpots with water. And they filled them up to the brim.
verses.bsb: Jesus told the servants, “Fill the jars with water.”  So they filled them to the brim.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast. And they bare it.
verses.bsb: “Now draw some out,” He said, “and take it to the master of the banquet.”  They did so,

verses.kjv: When the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine, and knew not whence it was: (but the servants which drew the water knew;) the governor of the feast called the bridegroom,
verses.bsb: and the master of the banquet tasted the water that had been turned into wine. He did not know where it was from, but the servants who had drawn the water knew. Then he called the bridegroom aside

verses.kjv: And saith unto him, Every man at the beginning doth set forth good wine; and when men have well drunk, then that which is worse: but thou hast kept the good wine until now.
verses.bsb: and said, “Everyone serves the fine wine first, and then the cheap wine after the guests are drunk. But you have saved the fine wine until now!”

verses.kjv: This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory; and his disciples believed on him.
verses.bsb: Jesus performed this, the first of His signs, at Cana in Galilee. He thus revealed His glory, and His disciples believed in Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ After this he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, and his brethren, and his disciples: and they continued there not many days.
verses.bsb: After this, He went down to Capernaum with His mother and brothers and His disciples, and they stayed there a few days.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Jews’ passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem,
verses.bsb: When the Jewish Passover was near, Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: And found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the changers of money sitting:
verses.bsb: In the temple courts He found men selling cattle, sheep, and doves, and money changers seated at their tables.

verses.kjv: And when he had made a scourge of small cords, he drove them all out of the temple, and the sheep, and the oxen; and poured out the changers’ money, and overthrew the tables;
verses.bsb: So He made a whip out of cords and drove all from the temple courts, both sheep and cattle. He poured out the coins of the money changers and overturned their tables.

verses.kjv: And said unto them that sold doves, Take these things hence; make not my Father’s house an house of merchandise.
verses.bsb: To those selling doves He said, “Get these out of here! How dare you turn My Father’s house into a marketplace!”

verses.kjv: And his disciples remembered that it was written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up.
verses.bsb: His disciples remembered that it is written: “Zeal for Your house will consume Me.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Then answered the Jews and said unto him, What sign shewest thou unto us, seeing that thou doest these things?
verses.bsb: On account of this, the Jews demanded, “What sign can You show us to prove Your authority to do these things?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up again.”

verses.kjv: Then said the Jews, Forty and six years was this temple in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?
verses.bsb: “This temple took forty-six years to build,” the Jews replied, “and You are going to raise it up in three days?”

verses.kjv: But he spake of the temple of his body.
verses.bsb: But Jesus was speaking about the temple of His body.

verses.kjv: When therefore he was risen from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this unto them; and they believed the scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.
verses.bsb: After He was raised from the dead, His disciples remembered that He had said this. Then they believed the Scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now when he was in Jerusalem at the passover, in the feast day, many believed in his name, when they saw the miracles which he did.
verses.bsb: While He was in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, many people saw the signs He was doing and believed in His name.

verses.kjv: But Jesus did not commit himself unto them, because he knew all men,
verses.bsb: But Jesus did not entrust Himself to them, for He knew them all.

verses.kjv: And needed not that any should testify of man: for he knew what was in man.
verses.bsb: He did not need any testimony about man, for He knew what was in a man.

verses.kjv: THERE was a man of the Pharisees, named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews:
verses.bsb: Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a leader of the Jews.

verses.kjv: The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these miracles that thou doest, except God be with him.
verses.bsb: He came to Jesus at night and said, “Rabbi, we know that You are a teacher who has come from God. For no one could perform the signs You are doing if God were not with him.”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born again, he cannot see the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God unless he is born again.”

verses.kjv: Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?
verses.bsb: “How can a man be born when he is old?” Nicodemus asked. “Can he enter his mother’s womb a second time to be born?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be born of water and of the Spirit, he cannot enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “Truly, truly, I tell you, no one can enter the kingdom of God unless he is born of water and the Spirit.

verses.kjv: That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
verses.bsb: Flesh is born of flesh, but spirit is born of the Spirit.

verses.kjv: Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be born again.
verses.bsb: Do not be amazed that I said, ‘You must be born again.’

verses.kjv: The wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Spirit.
verses.bsb: The wind blows where it wishes. You hear its sound, but you do not know where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.”

verses.kjv: Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?
verses.bsb: “How can this be?” Nicodemus asked.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?
verses.bsb: “You are Israel’s teacher,” said Jesus, “and you do not understand these things?

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, we speak of what we know, and we testify to what we have seen, and yet you people do not accept our testimony.

verses.kjv: If I have told you earthly things, and ye believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?
verses.bsb: If I have told you about earthly things and you do not believe, how will you believe if I tell you about heavenly things?

verses.kjv: And no man hath ascended up to heaven, but he that came down from heaven, even the Son of man which is in heaven.
verses.bsb: No one has ascended into heaven except the One who descended from heaven—the Son of Man.

verses.kjv: ¶ And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of man be lifted up:
verses.bsb: Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness, so the Son of Man must be lifted up,

verses.kjv: That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
verses.bsb: that everyone who believes in Him may have eternal life.

verses.kjv: ¶ For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son, that whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have everlasting life.
verses.bsb: For God so loved the world that He gave His one and only Son, that everyone who believes in Him shall not perish but have eternal life.

verses.kjv: For God sent not his Son into the world to condemn the world; but that the world through him might be saved.
verses.bsb: For God did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the name of the only begotten Son of God.
verses.bsb: Whoever believes in Him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe has already been condemned, because he has not believed in the name of God’s one and only Son.

verses.kjv: And this is the condemnation, that light is come into the world, and men loved darkness rather than light, because their deeds were evil.
verses.bsb: And this is the verdict: The Light has come into the world, but men loved the darkness rather than the Light because their deeds were evil.

verses.kjv: For every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.
verses.bsb: Everyone who does evil hates the Light, and does not come into the Light for fear that his deeds will be exposed.

verses.kjv: But he that doeth truth cometh to the light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.
verses.bsb: But whoever practices the truth comes into the Light, so that it may be seen clearly that what he has done has been accomplished in God.”

verses.kjv: ¶ After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judæa; and there he tarried with them, and baptized.
verses.bsb: After this, Jesus and His disciples went into the Judean countryside, where He spent some time with them and baptized.

verses.kjv: ¶ And John also was baptizing in Ænon near to Salim, because there was much water there: and they came, and were baptized.
verses.bsb: Now John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because the water was plentiful there, and people kept coming to be baptized.

verses.kjv: For John was not yet cast into prison.
verses.bsb: (For John had not yet been thrown into prison.)

verses.kjv: ¶ Then there arose a question between some of John’s disciples and the Jews about purifying.
verses.bsb: Then a dispute arose between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over the issue of ceremonial washing.

verses.kjv: And they came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, and all men come to him.
verses.bsb: So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Look, Rabbi, the One who was with you beyond the Jordan, the One you testified about—He is baptizing, and everyone is going to Him.”

verses.kjv: John answered and said, A man can receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
verses.bsb: John replied, “A man can receive only what is given him from heaven.

verses.kjv: Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
verses.bsb: You yourselves can testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ, but am sent ahead of Him.’

verses.kjv: He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
verses.bsb: The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom stands and listens for him, and is overjoyed to hear the bridegroom’s voice. That joy is mine, and it is now complete.

verses.kjv: He must increase, but I must decrease.
verses.bsb: He must increase; I must decrease.

verses.kjv: He that cometh from above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.
verses.bsb: The One who comes from above is above all. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The One who comes from heaven is above all.

verses.kjv: And what he hath seen and heard, that he testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
verses.bsb: He testifies to what He has seen and heard, yet no one accepts His testimony.

verses.kjv: He that hath received his testimony hath set to his seal that God is true.
verses.bsb: Whoever accepts His testimony has certified that God is truthful.

verses.kjv: For he whom God hath sent speaketh the words of God: for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
verses.bsb: For the One whom God has sent speaks the words of God, for God gives the Spirit without limit.

verses.kjv: The Father loveth the Son, and hath given all things into his hand.
verses.bsb: The Father loves the Son and has placed all things in His hands.

verses.kjv: He that believeth on the Son hath everlasting life: and he that believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the wrath of God abideth on him.
verses.bsb: Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever rejects the Son will not see life. Instead, the wrath of God remains on him.”

verses.kjv: WHEN therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,
verses.bsb: When Jesus realized that the Pharisees were aware He was gaining and baptizing more disciples than John

verses.kjv: (Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples,)
verses.bsb: (although it was not Jesus who baptized, but His disciples),

verses.kjv: He left Judæa, and departed again into Galilee.
verses.bsb: He left Judea and returned to Galilee.

verses.kjv: And he must needs go through Samaria.
verses.bsb: Now He had to pass through Samaria.

verses.kjv: Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.
verses.bsb: So He came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the plot of ground that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.

verses.kjv: Now Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well: and it was about the sixth hour.
verses.bsb: Since Jacob’s well was there, Jesus, weary from His journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour.

verses.kjv: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.
verses.bsb: When a Samaritan woman came to draw water, Jesus said to her, “Give Me a drink.”

verses.kjv: (For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)
verses.bsb: (His disciples had gone into the town to buy food.)

verses.kjv: Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.
verses.bsb: “You are a Jew,” said the woman. “How can You ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews do not associate with Samaritans.)

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “If you knew the gift of God and who is asking you for a drink, you would have asked Him, and He would have given you living water.”

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
verses.bsb: “Sir,” the woman replied, “You have nothing to draw with and the well is deep. Where then will You get this living water?

verses.kjv: Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?
verses.bsb: Are You greater than our father Jacob, who gave us this well and drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:
verses.bsb: Jesus said to her, “Everyone who drinks this water will be thirsty again.

verses.kjv: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.
verses.bsb: But whoever drinks the water I give him will never thirst. Indeed, the water I give him will become in him a fount of water springing up to eternal life.”

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.
verses.bsb: The woman said to Him, “Sir, give me this water so that I will not get thirsty and have to keep coming here to draw water.”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.
verses.bsb: Jesus told her, “Go, call your husband and come back.”

verses.kjv: The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no husband:
verses.bsb: “I have no husband,” the woman replied.  Jesus said to her, “You are correct to say that you have no husband.

verses.kjv: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.
verses.bsb: In fact, you have had five husbands, and the man you now have is not your husband. You have spoken truthfully.”

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.
verses.bsb: “Sir,” the woman said, “I see that You are a prophet.

verses.kjv: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.
verses.bsb: Our fathers worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say that the place where one must worship is in Jerusalem.”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.
verses.bsb: “Believe Me, woman,” Jesus replied, “a time is coming when you will worship the Father neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.
verses.bsb: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we do know, for salvation is from the Jews.

verses.kjv: But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.
verses.bsb: But a time is coming and has now come when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth, for the Father is seeking such as these to worship Him.

verses.kjv: God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.
verses.bsb: God is Spirit, and His worshipers must worship Him in spirit and in truth.”

verses.kjv: The woman saith unto him, I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things.
verses.bsb: The woman said, “I know that Messiah” (called Christ) “is coming. When He comes, He will explain everything to us.”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am he.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “I who speak to you am He.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And upon this came his disciples, and marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?
verses.bsb: Just then His disciples returned and were surprised that He was speaking with a woman. But no one asked Him, “What do You want from her?” or “Why are You talking with her?”

verses.kjv: The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men,
verses.bsb: Then the woman left her water jar, went back into the town, and said to the people,

verses.kjv: Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?
verses.bsb: “Come, see a man who told me everything I ever did. Could this be the Christ?”

verses.kjv: Then they went out of the city, and came unto him.
verses.bsb: So they left the town and made their way toward Jesus.

verses.kjv: ¶ In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile the disciples urged Him, “Rabbi, eat something.”

verses.kjv: But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.
verses.bsb: But He told them, “I have food to eat that you know nothing about.”

verses.kjv: Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?
verses.bsb: So the disciples asked one another, “Could someone have brought Him food?”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.
verses.bsb: Jesus explained, “My food is to do the will of Him who sent Me and to finish His work.

verses.kjv: Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.
verses.bsb: Do you not say, ‘There are still four months until the harvest’? I tell you, lift up your eyes and look at the fields, for they are ripe for harvest.

verses.kjv: And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.
verses.bsb: Already the reaper draws his wages and gathers a crop for eternal life, so that the sower and the reaper may rejoice together.

verses.kjv: And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.
verses.bsb: For in this case the saying ‘One sows and another reaps’ is true.

verses.kjv: I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.
verses.bsb: I sent you to reap what you have not worked for; others have done the hard work, and now you have taken up their labor.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did.
verses.bsb: Many of the Samaritans from that town believed in Jesus because of the woman’s testimony, “He told me everything I ever did.”

verses.kjv: So when the Samaritans were come unto him, they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days.
verses.bsb: So when the Samaritans came to Him, they asked Him to stay with them, and He stayed two days.

verses.kjv: And many more believed because of his own word;
verses.bsb: And many more believed because of His message.

verses.kjv: And said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.
verses.bsb: They said to the woman, “We now believe not only because of your words; we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this man truly is the Savior of the world.”

verses.kjv: ¶ Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
verses.bsb: After two days, Jesus left for Galilee.

verses.kjv: For Jesus himself testified, that a prophet hath no honour in his own country.
verses.bsb: Now He Himself had testified that a prophet has no honor in his own hometown.

verses.kjv: Then when he was come into Galilee, the Galilæans received him, having seen all the things that he did at Jerusalem at the feast: for they also went unto the feast.
verses.bsb: Yet when He arrived, the Galileans welcomed Him. They had seen all the great things He had done in Jerusalem at the feast, for they had gone there as well.

verses.kjv: So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.
verses.bsb: So once again He came to Cana in Galilee, where He had turned the water into wine. And there was a royal official whose son lay sick at Capernaum.

verses.kjv: When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judæa into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.
verses.bsb: When he heard that Jesus had come from Judea to Galilee, he went and begged Him to come down and heal his son, who was about to die.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to him, “Unless you people see signs and wonders, you will never believe.”

verses.kjv: The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.
verses.bsb: “Sir,” the official said, “come down before my child dies.”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
verses.bsb: “Go,” said Jesus. “Your son will live.”  The man took Jesus at His word and departed.

verses.kjv: And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.
verses.bsb: And while he was still on the way, his servants met him with the news that his boy was alive.

verses.kjv: Then inquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.
verses.bsb: So he inquired as to the hour when his son had recovered, and they told him, “The fever left him yesterday at the seventh hour.”

verses.kjv: So the father knew that it was at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and himself believed, and his whole house.
verses.bsb: Then the father realized that this was the very hour in which Jesus had told him, “Your son will live.” And he and all his household believed.

verses.kjv: This is again the second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judæa into Galilee.
verses.bsb: This was now the second sign that Jesus performed after coming from Judea into Galilee.

verses.kjv: AFTER this there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Some time later there was a feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.

verses.kjv: Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.
verses.bsb: Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool with five covered colonnades, which in Hebrew is called Bethesda.

verses.kjv: In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.
verses.bsb: On these walkways lay a great number of the sick, the blind, the lame, and the paralyzed.

verses.kjv: And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.
verses.bsb: One man there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.

verses.kjv: When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw him lying there and realized that he had spent a long time in this condition, He asked him, “Do you want to get well?”

verses.kjv: The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.
verses.bsb: “Sir,” the invalid replied, “I have no one to help me into the pool when the water is stirred. While I am on my way, someone else goes in before me.”

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto him, Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told him, “Get up, pick up your mat, and walk.”

verses.kjv: And immediately the man was made whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the sabbath.
verses.bsb: Immediately the man was made well, and he picked up his mat and began to walk.  Now this happened on the Sabbath day,

verses.kjv: ¶ The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed.
verses.bsb: so the Jews said to the man who had been healed, “This is the Sabbath! It is unlawful for you to carry your mat.”

verses.kjv: He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.
verses.bsb: But he answered, “The man who made me well told me, ‘Pick up your mat and walk.’”

verses.kjv: Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?
verses.bsb: “Who is this man who told you to pick it up and walk?” they asked.

verses.kjv: And he that was healed wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in that place.
verses.bsb: But the man who was healed did not know who it was, for Jesus had slipped away while the crowd was there.

verses.kjv: Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.
verses.bsb: Afterward, Jesus found the man at the temple and said to him, “See, you have been made well. Stop sinning, or something worse may happen to you.”

verses.kjv: The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.
verses.bsb: And the man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.

verses.kjv: And therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: Now because Jesus was doing these things on the Sabbath, the Jews began to persecute Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ But Jesus answered them, My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.
verses.bsb: But Jesus answered them, “To this very day My Father is at His work, and I too am working.”

verses.kjv: Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his Father, making himself equal with God.
verses.bsb: Because of this, the Jews tried all the harder to kill Him. Not only was He breaking the Sabbath, but He was even calling God His own Father, making Himself equal with God.

verses.kjv: Then answered Jesus and said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he seeth the Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.
verses.bsb: So Jesus replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you, the Son can do nothing by Himself, unless He sees the Father doing it. For whatever the Father does, the Son also does.

verses.kjv: For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.
verses.bsb: The Father loves the Son and shows Him all He does. And to your amazement, He will show Him even greater works than these.

verses.kjv: For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.
verses.bsb: For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life, so also the Son gives life to whom He wishes.

verses.kjv: For the Father judgeth no man, but hath committed all judgment unto the Son:
verses.bsb: Furthermore, the Father judges no one, but has assigned all judgment to the Son,

verses.kjv: That all men should honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.
verses.bsb: so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting life, and shall not come into condemnation; but is passed from death unto life.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever hears My word and believes Him who sent Me has eternal life and will not come under judgment. Indeed, he has crossed over from death to life.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the dead shall hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall live.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, the hour is coming and has now come when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear will live.

verses.kjv: For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the Son to have life in himself;
verses.bsb: For as the Father has life in Himself, so also He has granted the Son to have life in Himself.

verses.kjv: And hath given him authority to execute judgment also, because he is the Son of man.
verses.bsb: And He has given Him authority to execute judgment, because He is the Son of Man.

verses.kjv: Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the graves shall hear his voice,
verses.bsb: Do not be amazed at this, for the hour is coming when all who are in their graves will hear His voice

verses.kjv: And shall come forth; they that have done good, unto the resurrection of life; and they that have done evil, unto the resurrection of damnation.
verses.bsb: and come out—those who have done good to the resurrection of life, and those who have done evil to the resurrection of judgment.

verses.kjv: I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is just; because I seek not mine own will, but the will of the Father which hath sent me.
verses.bsb: I can do nothing by Myself; I judge only as I hear. And My judgment is just, because I do not seek My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.

verses.kjv: If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.
verses.bsb: If I testify about Myself, My testimony is not valid.

verses.kjv: ¶ There is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
verses.bsb: There is another who testifies about Me, and I know that His testimony about Me is valid.

verses.kjv: Ye sent unto John, and he bare witness unto the truth.
verses.bsb: You have sent to John, and he has testified to the truth.

verses.kjv: But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.
verses.bsb: Even though I do not accept human testimony, I say these things so that you may be saved.

verses.kjv: He was a burning and a shining light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light.
verses.bsb: John was a lamp that burned and gave light, and you were willing for a season to bask in his light.

verses.kjv: ¶ But I have greater witness than that of John: for the works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath sent me.
verses.bsb: But I have testimony more substantial than that of John. For the works that the Father has given Me to accomplish—the very works I am doing—testify about Me that the Father has sent Me.

verses.kjv: And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor seen his shape.
verses.bsb: And the Father who sent Me has Himself testified about Me. You have never heard His voice nor seen His form,

verses.kjv: And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye believe not.
verses.bsb: nor does His word abide in you, because you do not believe the One He sent.

verses.kjv: ¶ Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which testify of me.
verses.bsb: You pore over the Scriptures because you presume that by them you possess eternal life. These are the very words that testify about Me,

verses.kjv: And ye will not come to me, that ye might have life.
verses.bsb: yet you refuse to come to Me to have life.

verses.kjv: I receive not honour from men.
verses.bsb: I do not accept glory from men,

verses.kjv: But I know you, that ye have not the love of God in you.
verses.bsb: but I know you, that you do not have the love of God within you.

verses.kjv: I am come in my Father’s name, and ye receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.
verses.bsb: I have come in My Father’s name, and you have not received Me; but if someone else comes in his own name, you will receive him.

verses.kjv: How can ye believe, which receive honour one of another, and seek not the honour that cometh from God only?
verses.bsb: How can you believe if you accept glory from one another, yet do not seek the glory that comes from the only God?

verses.kjv: Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.
verses.bsb: Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses, in whom you have put your hope.

verses.kjv: For had ye believed Moses, ye would have believed me: for he wrote of me.
verses.bsb: If you had believed Moses, you would believe Me, because he wrote about Me.

verses.kjv: But if ye believe not his writings, how shall ye believe my words?
verses.bsb: But since you do not believe what he wrote, how will you believe what I say?”

verses.kjv: AFTER these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.
verses.bsb: After this, Jesus crossed to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (that is, the Sea of Tiberias).

verses.kjv: And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.
verses.bsb: A large crowd followed Him because they saw the signs He was performing on the sick.

verses.kjv: And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus went up on the mountain and sat down with His disciples.

verses.kjv: And the passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.
verses.bsb: Now the Jewish Feast of the Passover was near.

verses.kjv: ¶ When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?
verses.bsb: When Jesus looked up and saw a large crowd coming toward Him, He said to Philip, “Where can we buy bread for these people to eat?”

verses.kjv: And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
verses.bsb: But He was asking this to test him, for He knew what He was about to do.

verses.kjv: Philip answered him, Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.
verses.bsb: Philip answered, “Two hundred denarii would not buy enough bread for each of them to have a small piece.”

verses.kjv: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, saith unto him,
verses.bsb: One of His disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to Him,

verses.kjv: There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?
verses.bsb: “Here is a boy with five barley loaves and two small fish. But what difference will these make among so many?”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
verses.bsb: “Have the people sit down,” Jesus said. Now there was plenty of grass in that place, so the men sat down, about five thousand of them.

verses.kjv: And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were set down; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus took the loaves and the fish, gave thanks, and distributed to those who were seated as much as they wanted.

verses.kjv: When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be lost.
verses.bsb: And when everyone was full, He said to His disciples, “Gather the pieces that are left over, so that nothing will be wasted.”

verses.kjv: Therefore they gathered them together, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained over and above unto them that had eaten.
verses.bsb: So they collected them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves left over by those who had eaten.

verses.kjv: Then those men, when they had seen the miracle that Jesus did, said, This is of a truth that prophet that should come into the world.
verses.bsb: When the people saw the sign that Jesus had performed, they began to say, “Truly this is the Prophet who is to come into the world.”

verses.kjv: ¶ When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a king, he departed again into a mountain himself alone.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus, realizing that they were about to come and make Him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by Himself.

verses.kjv: And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,
verses.bsb: When evening came, His disciples went down to the sea,

verses.kjv: And entered into a ship, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was now dark, and Jesus was not come to them.
verses.bsb: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was already dark, and Jesus had not yet gone out to them.

verses.kjv: And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.
verses.bsb: A strong wind was blowing, and the sea grew agitated.

verses.kjv: So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were afraid.
verses.bsb: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus approaching the boat, walking on the sea—and they were terrified.

verses.kjv: But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.
verses.bsb: But Jesus spoke up: “It is I; do not be afraid.”

verses.kjv: Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.
verses.bsb: Then they were willing to take Him into the boat, and at once the boat reached the shore where they were heading.

verses.kjv: ¶ The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;
verses.bsb: The next day, the crowd that had remained on the other side of the sea realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not boarded it with His disciples, but they had gone away alone.

verses.kjv: (Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)
verses.bsb: However, some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.

verses.kjv: When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.
verses.bsb: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor His disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Him.

verses.kjv: And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?
verses.bsb: When they found Him on the other side of the sea, they asked Him, “Rabbi, when did You get here?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “Truly, truly, I tell you, it is not because you saw these signs that you are looking for Me, but because you ate the loaves and had your fill.

verses.kjv: Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father sealed.
verses.bsb: Do not work for food that perishes, but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For on Him God the Father has placed His seal of approval.”

verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
verses.bsb: Then they inquired, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “The work of God is this: to believe in the One He has sent.”

verses.kjv: They said therefore unto him, What sign shewest thou then, that we may see, and believe thee? what dost thou work?
verses.bsb: So they asked Him, “What sign then will You perform, so that we may see it and believe You? What will You do?

verses.kjv: Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them bread from heaven to eat.
verses.bsb: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, as it is written: ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is My Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.

verses.kjv: For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.
verses.bsb: For the bread of God is He who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”

verses.kjv: Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.
verses.bsb: “Sir,” they said, “give us this bread at all times.”

verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to Me will never hunger, and whoever believes in Me will never thirst.

verses.kjv: But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.
verses.bsb: But as I stated, you have seen Me and still you do not believe.

verses.kjv: All that the Father giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise cast out.
verses.bsb: Everyone the Father gives Me will come to Me, and the one who comes to Me I will never drive away.

verses.kjv: For I came down from heaven, not to do mine own will, but the will of him that sent me.
verses.bsb: For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but to do the will of Him who sent Me.

verses.kjv: And this is the Father’s will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up again at the last day.
verses.bsb: And this is the will of Him who sent Me, that I shall lose none of those He has given Me, but raise them up at the last day.

verses.kjv: And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and believeth on him, may have everlasting life: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.bsb: For it is My Father’s will that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in Him shall have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.”

verses.kjv: The Jews then murmured at him, because he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews began to grumble about Jesus because He had said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”

verses.kjv: And they said, Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? how is it then that he saith, I came down from heaven?
verses.bsb: They were asking, “Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then can He say, ‘I have come down from heaven?’”

verses.kjv: Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, Murmur not among yourselves.
verses.bsb: “Stop grumbling among yourselves,” Jesus replied.

verses.kjv: No man can come to me, except the Father which hath sent me draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.bsb: “No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.

verses.kjv: It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the Father, cometh unto me.
verses.bsb: It is written in the Prophets: ‘And they will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard the Father and learned from Him comes to Me—

verses.kjv: Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath seen the Father.
verses.bsb: not that anyone has seen the Father except the One who is from God; only He has seen the Father.

verses.kjv: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on me hath everlasting life.
verses.bsb: Truly, truly, I tell you, he who believes has eternal life.

verses.kjv: I am that bread of life.
verses.bsb: I am the bread of life.

verses.kjv: Your fathers did eat manna in the wilderness, and are dead.
verses.bsb: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, yet they died.

verses.kjv: This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.
verses.bsb: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that anyone may eat of it and not die.

verses.kjv: I am the living bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the bread that I will give is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.
verses.bsb: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. And this bread, which I will give for the life of the world, is My flesh.”

verses.kjv: The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews began to argue among themselves, “How can this man give us His flesh to eat?”

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye eat the flesh of the Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.
verses.bsb: So Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man, you have no life in you.

verses.kjv: Whoso eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, hath eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.
verses.bsb: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.

verses.kjv: For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
verses.bsb: For My flesh is real food, and My blood is real drink.

verses.kjv: He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.
verses.bsb: Whoever eats My flesh and drinks My blood remains in Me, and I in him.

verses.kjv: As the living Father hath sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall live by me.
verses.bsb: Just as the living Father sent Me and I live because of the Father, so also the one who feeds on Me will live because of Me.

verses.kjv: This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.
verses.bsb: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Unlike your fathers, who ate the manna and died, the one who eats this bread will live forever.”

verses.kjv: These things said he in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
verses.bsb: Jesus said this while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.

verses.kjv: Many therefore of his disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is an hard saying; who can hear it?
verses.bsb: On hearing it, many of His disciples said, “This is a difficult teaching. Who can accept it?”

verses.kjv: When Jesus knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it, he said unto them, Doth this offend you?
verses.bsb: Aware that His disciples were grumbling about this teaching, Jesus asked them, “Does this offend you?

verses.kjv: What and if ye shall see the Son of man ascend up where he was before?
verses.bsb: Then what will happen if you see the Son of Man ascend to where He was before?

verses.kjv: It is the spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.
verses.bsb: The Spirit gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and they are life.

verses.kjv: But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should betray him.
verses.bsb: However, there are some of you who do not believe.” (For Jesus had known from the beginning which of them did not believe and who would betray Him.)

verses.kjv: And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, except it were given unto him of my Father.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said, “This is why I told you that no one can come to Me unless the Father has granted it to him.”

verses.kjv: ¶ From that time many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
verses.bsb: From that time on many of His disciples turned back and no longer walked with Him.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto the twelve, Will ye also go away?
verses.bsb: So Jesus asked the Twelve, “Do you want to leave too?”

verses.kjv: Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.
verses.bsb: Simon Peter replied, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.

verses.kjv: And we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the Son of the living God.
verses.bsb: We believe and know that You are the Holy One of God.”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
verses.bsb: Jesus answered them, “Have I not chosen you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil!”

verses.kjv: He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.
verses.bsb: He was speaking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. For although Judas was one of the Twelve, he was later to betray Jesus.

verses.kjv: AFTER these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews sought to kill him.
verses.bsb: After this, Jesus traveled throughout Galilee. He did not want to travel in Judea, because the Jews there were trying to kill Him.

verses.kjv: Now the Jews’ feast of tabernacles was at hand.
verses.bsb: However, the Jewish Feast of Tabernacles was near.

verses.kjv: His brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judæa, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.
verses.bsb: So Jesus’ brothers said to Him, “Leave here and go to Judea, so that Your disciples there may see the works You are doing.

verses.kjv: For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.
verses.bsb: For no one who wants to be known publicly acts in secret. Since You are doing these things, show Yourself to the world.”

verses.kjv: For neither did his brethren believe in him.
verses.bsb: For even His own brothers did not believe in Him.

verses.kjv: Then Jesus said unto them, My time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.
verses.bsb: Therefore Jesus told them, “Although your time is always at hand, My time has not yet come.

verses.kjv: The world cannot hate you; but me it hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are evil.
verses.bsb: The world cannot hate you, but it hates Me, because I testify that its works are evil.

verses.kjv: Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.
verses.bsb: Go up to the feast on your own. I am not going up to this feast, because My time has not yet come.”

verses.kjv: When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
verses.bsb: Having said this, Jesus remained in Galilee.

verses.kjv: ¶ But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
verses.bsb: But after His brothers had gone up to the feast, He also went—not publicly, but in secret.

verses.kjv: Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?
verses.bsb: So the Jews were looking for Him at the feast and asking, “Where is He?”

verses.kjv: And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.
verses.bsb: Many in the crowds were whispering about Him. Some said, “He is a good man.”  But others replied, “No, He deceives the people.”

verses.kjv: Howbeit no man spake openly of him for fear of the Jews.
verses.bsb: Yet no one would speak publicly about Him for fear of the Jews.

verses.kjv: ¶ Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
verses.bsb: About halfway through the feast, Jesus went up to the temple courts and began to teach.

verses.kjv: And the Jews marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?
verses.bsb: The Jews were amazed and asked, “How did this man attain such learning without having studied?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered them, and said, My doctrine is not mine, but his that sent me.
verses.bsb: “My teaching is not My own,” Jesus replied. “It comes from Him who sent Me.

verses.kjv: If any man will do his will, he shall know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.
verses.bsb: If anyone desires to do His will, he will know whether My teaching is from God or whether I speak on My own.

verses.kjv: He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own glory: but he that seeketh his glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.
verses.bsb: He who speaks on his own authority seeks his own glory, but He who seeks the glory of the One who sent Him is a man of truth; in Him there is no falsehood.

verses.kjv: Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?
verses.bsb: Has not Moses given you the law? Yet not one of you keeps it. Why are you trying to kill Me?”

verses.kjv: The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?
verses.bsb: “You have a demon,” the crowd replied. “Who is trying to kill You?”

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered them, “I did one miracle, and you are all amazed.

verses.kjv: Moses therefore gave unto you circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.
verses.bsb: But because Moses gave you circumcision, you circumcise a boy on the Sabbath (not that it is from Moses, but from the patriarchs.)

verses.kjv: If a man on the sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit whole on the sabbath day?
verses.bsb: If a boy can be circumcised on the Sabbath so that the law of Moses will not be broken, why are you angry with Me for making the whole man well on the Sabbath?

verses.kjv: Judge not according to the appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
verses.bsb: Stop judging by outward appearances, and start judging justly.”

verses.kjv: Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?
verses.bsb: Then some of the people of Jerusalem began to say, “Isn’t this the man they are trying to kill?

verses.kjv: But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?
verses.bsb: Yet here He is, speaking publicly, and they are not saying anything to Him. Have the rulers truly recognized that this is the Christ?

verses.kjv: Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.
verses.bsb: But we know where this man is from. When the Christ comes, no one will know where He is from.”

verses.kjv: Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is true, whom ye know not.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus, still teaching in the temple courts, cried out, “You know Me, and you know where I am from. I have not come of My own accord, but He who sent Me is true. You do not know Him,

verses.kjv: But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath sent me.
verses.bsb: but I know Him, because I am from Him and He sent Me.”

verses.kjv: Then they sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his hour was not yet come.
verses.bsb: So they tried to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him, because His hour had not yet come.

verses.kjv: And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
verses.bsb: Many in the crowd, however, believed in Him and said, “When the Christ comes, will He perform more signs than this man?”

verses.kjv: ¶ The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
verses.bsb: When the Pharisees heard the crowd whispering these things about Jesus, they and the chief priests sent officers to arrest Him.

verses.kjv: Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
verses.bsb: So Jesus said, “I am with you only a little while longer, and then I am going to the One who sent Me.

verses.kjv: Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
verses.bsb: You will look for Me, but you will not find Me; and where I am, you cannot come.”

verses.kjv: Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?
verses.bsb: At this, the Jews said to one another, “Where does He intend to go that we will not find Him? Will He go where the Jews are dispersed among the Greeks, and teach the Greeks?

verses.kjv: What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?
verses.bsb: What does He mean by saying, ‘You will look for Me, but you will not find Me,’ and, ‘Where I am, you cannot come’?”

verses.kjv: In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man thirst, let him come unto me, and drink.
verses.bsb: On the last and greatest day of the feast, Jesus stood up and called out in a loud voice, “If anyone is thirsty, let him come to Me and drink.

verses.kjv: He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.
verses.bsb: Whoever believes in Me, as the Scripture has said: ‘Streams of living water will flow from within him.’”

verses.kjv: (But this spake he of the Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)
verses.bsb: He was speaking about the Spirit, whom those who believed in Him were later to receive. For the Spirit had not yet been given, because Jesus had not yet been glorified.

verses.kjv: ¶ Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the Prophet.
verses.bsb: On hearing these words, some of the people said, “This is truly the Prophet.”

verses.kjv: Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?
verses.bsb: Others declared, “This is the Christ.”  But still others asked, “How can the Christ come from Galilee?

verses.kjv: Hath not the scripture said, That Christ cometh of the seed of David, and out of the town of Bethlehem, where David was?
verses.bsb: Doesn’t the Scripture say that the Christ will come from the line of David and from Bethlehem, the village where David lived?”

verses.kjv: So there was a division among the people because of him.
verses.bsb: So there was division in the crowd because of Jesus.

verses.kjv: And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
verses.bsb: Some of them wanted to seize Him, but no one laid a hand on Him.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?
verses.bsb: Then the officers returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, who asked them, “Why didn’t you bring Him in?”

verses.kjv: The officers answered, Never man spake like this man.
verses.bsb: “Never has anyone spoken like this man!” the officers answered.

verses.kjv: Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also deceived?
verses.bsb: “Have you also been deceived?” replied the Pharisees.

verses.kjv: Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
verses.bsb: “Have any of the rulers or Pharisees believed in Him?

verses.kjv: But this people who knoweth not the law are cursed.
verses.bsb: But this crowd that does not know the law, they are under a curse.”

verses.kjv: Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)
verses.bsb: Nicodemus, who had gone to Jesus earlier and who himself was one of them, asked,

verses.kjv: Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?
verses.bsb: “Does our law convict a man without first hearing from him to determine what he has done?”

verses.kjv: They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
verses.bsb: “Aren’t you also from Galilee?” they replied. “Look into it, and you will see that no prophet comes out of Galilee.”

verses.kjv: And every man went unto his own house.
verses.bsb: Then each went to his own home.

verses.kjv: JESUS went unto the mount of Olives.
verses.bsb: But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.

verses.kjv: And early in the morning he came again into the temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
verses.bsb: Early in the morning He went back into the temple courts. All the people came to Him, and He sat down to teach them.

verses.kjv: And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a woman taken in adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,
verses.bsb: The scribes and Pharisees, however, brought to Him a woman caught in adultery. They made her stand before them

verses.kjv: They say unto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.
verses.bsb: and said, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery.

verses.kjv: Now Moses in the law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?
verses.bsb: In the Law Moses commanded us to stone such a woman. So what do You say?”

verses.kjv: This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.
verses.bsb: They said this to test Him, in order to have a basis for accusing Him. But Jesus bent down and began to write on the ground with His finger.

verses.kjv: So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, He that is without sin among you, let him first cast a stone at her.
verses.bsb: When they continued to question Him, He straightened up and said to them, “Let him who is without sin among you be the first to cast a stone at her.”

verses.kjv: And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.
verses.bsb: And again He bent down and wrote on the ground.

verses.kjv: And they which heard it, being convicted by their own conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
verses.bsb: When they heard this, they began to go away one by one, beginning with the older ones, until only Jesus was left, with the woman standing there.
